hypothesis: Pre-training generative models on large chemical datasets followed by fine-tuning on the curated 103 CTF structures and reinforcement learning with expert-defined structural reward functions will produce porous carbon candidates that pass a higher percentage of expert filters and show greater validity, novelty, and internal diversity (and lower Fréchet ChemNet Distance) than models that use pre-training alone or omit domain-specific fine-tuning and reward optimization.
